reason: The passage contains multiple named characters with specific professions and dialogue. Aaron (אַהֲרֹן) is explicitly named and serves as a religious leader with extensive dialogue, including 'וַיֹּאמֶר אֲלֵהֶם אַהֲרֹן' (And Aaron said to them). Moses (מֹשֶׁה) is named, functions as a leader and prophet, and has multiple dialogues such as 'וַיֹּאמֶר מֹשֶׁה אֶֽל־ אַהֲרֹן' (And Moses said to Aaron). Joshua (יְהוֹשֻׁעַ) is named, serves as a military aide, and speaks: 'וַיֹּאמֶר אֶל־ מֹשֶׁה' (and he said to Moses). Additionally, the people collectively are portrayed as characters engaging in dialogue ('וַיֹּאמְרוּ' - and they said), and the Levites appear as a professional group receiving dialogue. These are regular human characters depicted with names, professions/roles, and direct speech.
